item.title: Lilly Kelonia Therapeutics Acquisition
item.description:
• Summary: Eli Lilly has acquired Kelonia Therapeutics to bolster its capabilities in in vivo gene delivery technologies. The acquisition, described by Lilly's CEO as part of a 'frenzied feeding' strategy, is expected to enhance the company's pipeline in genetic medicine and oncology. Kelonia's platform enables precise delivery of therapeutic genes directly into cells, potentially expanding Lilly's reach beyond traditional small molecules and biologics.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News, Fierce Biotech

item.title: Pfizer Cost-Cutting and Layoffs
item.description:
• Summary: Pfizer continues its cost-cutting efforts by planning manufacturing layoffs in Ireland, as part of a broader restructuring program. The move follows previous reductions and reflects the company's strategy to streamline operations amid changing market conditions and post-pandemic revenue normalization. The layoffs are expected to affect hundreds of employees at its Irish facilities.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Daiichi Sankyo Annual Report Delay
item.description:
• Summary: Daiichi Sankyo has postponed its annual report, citing extended reviews amid changing business conditions. The delay caused shares to slip as investors grew cautious. The company stated that additional time is needed to finalize the report due to ongoing assessments of its business environment and potential impacts on financial forecasts. The delay has raised questions about the company's near-term outlook.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma, BioSpace, Endpoints News

item.title: Regeneron Drug Pricing and White House Deal
item.description:
• Summary: Regeneron has reached a deal with the Trump administration on drug pricing, becoming the latest company to join the administration's push to lower costs. The agreement coincides with Regeneron also securing a landmark approval for one of its therapies. The deal may involve commitments to limit price increases or provide discounts, part of broader White House efforts to address drug affordability.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News, BioSpace

item.title: Helus Pharma CEO Change
item.description:
• Summary: Helus Pharma has replaced its CEO after only two months, adding an experienced executive to its science board. The sudden leadership shift comes as the company navigates its early-stage pipeline development. Separately, Pfizer's strategy chief Andrew Baum also stepped down, indicating a broader wave of executive moves in the biopharma sector.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Biotech, Endpoints News

item.title: AbbVie Botox CRL
item.description:
• Summary: AbbVie received a Complete Response Letter (CRL) from the FDA for a new prospective Botox (onabotulinumtoxinA) offering. The CRL cited manufacturing-related issues, effectively blocking the company's plans to expand its Botox product line. The setback highlights regulatory challenges in bringing new formulations of established products to market.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Pharmalot Job Moves
item.description:
• Summary: The latest Pharmalot column rounds up executive job changes in the pharmaceutical industry, including appointments, departures, and promotions across multiple companies. These movers reflect ongoing organizational shifts and strategic realignments within the sector.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News
